_comment_overview: BertCrossChunkExtractor gives tokens cross-chunk context: Pass 1 encodes each chunk with BERT, then Pass 2 runs cross-chunk transformer layers where each chunk's tokens attend to [CLS] embeddings from ALL other chunks. This lets tokens see global document context while keeping per-chunk computation tractable.

architecture._comment_chunk_encoder: BERT model for encoding individual chunks. prajjwal1/bert-tiny is fast (4.4M params). For better quality, try prajjwal1/bert-small or distilbert-base-uncased.
architecture._comment_freeze: Whether to freeze BERT weights. False allows end-to-end fine-tuning (recommended for best performance).
architecture._comment_max_chunks: Maximum chunks per document. Documents with more chunks are truncated.
architecture._comment_chunk_size: Number of tokens per chunk for BERT encoding.
architecture._comment_chunk_overlap: Number of overlapping tokens between adjacent chunks for context continuity.
architecture._comment_cross_layers: Number of cross-chunk transformer layers. More layers = more cross-chunk interaction but slower.
architecture._comment_attention_heads: Number of attention heads in cross-chunk transformer layers.
architecture._comment_cross_chunk_dim: Hidden dimension for cross-chunk transformer. Should be divisible by num_attention_heads.
architecture._comment_dropout: Dropout rate in cross-chunk transformer layers.
architecture._comment_gated_attention_dim: Hidden dimension for the gated attention pooling that aggregates chunks into a document vector.
architecture._comment_projection_dim: Final output dimension of the feature extractor. This feeds into the causal head.
